chunks.title: What is DFA in TOC with examples in hindi 480p
chunks.text: Hello friends, welcome to Gate Smashers.

chunks.title: What is DFA in TOC with examples in hindi 480p
chunks.text: In this video I am going to explain DFA i.e. Deterministic Finite Automata

chunks.title: What is DFA in TOC with examples in hindi 480p
chunks.text: and I am going to discuss all the important points related to it

chunks.title: What is DFA in TOC with examples in hindi 480p
chunks.text: which will be very beneficial for your competitive exams,

chunks.title: What is DFA in TOC with examples in hindi 480p
chunks.text: even for your college or university level exams.

chunks.title: What is DFA in TOC with examples in hindi 480p
chunks.text: So guys quickly like the video, subscribe to the channel if you haven't done it yet

chunks.title: What is DFA in TOC with examples in hindi 480p
chunks.text: and please press the bell button so that you get all the latest notifications.

chunks.title: What is DFA in TOC with examples in hindi 480p
chunks.text: So let's start DFA i.e. Deterministic Finite Automata.

chunks.title: What is DFA in TOC with examples in hindi 480p
chunks.text: Now in the first important point I will tell you what is the meaning of deterministic.

chunks.title: What is DFA in TOC with examples in hindi 480p
chunks.text: What is the meaning of deterministic here is that if I have a state in Automata Q0

chunks.title: What is DFA in TOC with examples in hindi 480p
chunks.text: and I have applied any string on Q0, I have applied any alphabet on Q0,

chunks.title: What is DFA in TOC with examples in hindi 480p
chunks.text: let's say I have applied A, whether you do A, B, C, any alphabet you have applied

chunks.title: What is DFA in TOC with examples in hindi 480p
chunks.text: then it will go to a particular state.

chunks.title: What is DFA in TOC with examples in hindi 480p
chunks.text: Now what will be that state? It can be Q1, Q2, Qn, Q0 but there will be one state,

chunks.title: What is DFA in TOC with examples in hindi 480p
chunks.text: this is fixed and where this thing gets fixed, we call it deterministic

chunks.title: What is DFA in TOC with examples in hindi 480p
chunks.text: which is not there in the case of NFA because in the case of NFA,

chunks.title: What is DFA in TOC with examples in hindi 480p
chunks.text: it can go to Q1, Q2, Q3, it can go to multiple states,

chunks.title: What is DFA in TOC with examples in hindi 480p
chunks.text: it can go to null state, it can go to any state,

chunks.title: What is DFA in TOC with examples in hindi 480p
chunks.text: so there are multiple choices in the case of NFA but deterministic finite automata means

chunks.title: What is DFA in TOC with examples in hindi 480p
chunks.text: that if you apply any alphabet on a state then it will take you to a particular single state.

chunks.title: What is DFA in TOC with examples in hindi 480p
chunks.text: So this is the meaning of deterministic.

chunks.title: What is DFA in TOC with examples in hindi 480p
chunks.text: And what is Automata? We have already discussed Automata,

chunks.title: What is DFA in TOC with examples in hindi 480p
chunks.text: what we are calling Automata is a machine which is actually using the representation of language.

chunks.title: What is DFA in TOC with examples in hindi 480p
chunks.text: Means if we have to represent language in standard way,

chunks.title: What is DFA in TOC with examples in hindi 480p
chunks.text: whether my language is finite or infinite,

chunks.title: What is DFA in TOC with examples in hindi 480p
chunks.text: if I have to represent it then what I use? I use Automata.

chunks.title: What is DFA in TOC with examples in hindi 480p
chunks.text: So Automata is a machine, an abstract model which actually tells

chunks.title: What is DFA in TOC with examples in hindi 480p
chunks.text: whether this language will be accepted or not.

chunks.title: What is DFA in TOC with examples in hindi 480p
chunks.text: So this is called deterministic finite automata.

chunks.title: What is DFA in TOC with examples in hindi 480p
chunks.text: Now how do we denote DFA? We denote DFA with these 5 symbols,

chunks.title: What is DFA in TOC with examples in hindi 480p
chunks.text: we actually denote it with these 5 tuples.

chunks.title: What is DFA in TOC with examples in hindi 480p
chunks.text: So what we are actually saying here is, first of all Q,

chunks.title: What is DFA in TOC with examples in hindi 480p
chunks.text: what is the meaning of capital Q here? Set of all finite states.

chunks.title: What is DFA in TOC with examples in hindi 480p
chunks.text: Set of finite states means what all states will be there in your machine.

chunks.title: What is DFA in TOC with examples in hindi 480p
chunks.text: What is the meaning of states?

chunks.title: What is DFA in TOC with examples in hindi 480p
chunks.text: That from one state, means starting state to go to output,

chunks.title: What is DFA in TOC with examples in hindi 480p
chunks.text: how many transitions you did, how many states you went to,

chunks.title: What is DFA in TOC with examples in hindi 480p
chunks.text: so why do we call the collection of all those states as Q?

chunks.title: What is DFA in TOC with examples in hindi 480p
chunks.text: Then we have summation here.

chunks.title: What is DFA in TOC with examples in hindi 480p
chunks.text: What is this actually? This is what I have already discussed,

chunks.title: What is DFA in TOC with examples in hindi 480p
chunks.text: this is alphabet, set of alphabet.

chunks.title: What is DFA in TOC with examples in hindi 480p
chunks.text: Let's suppose, what we take commonly, like we take A and B,

chunks.title: What is DFA in TOC with examples in hindi 480p
chunks.text: means where is it going on A, where is it going on B,

chunks.title: What is DFA in TOC with examples in hindi 480p
chunks.text: we take 0 and 1 also but most of the time we use A and B alphabet.

chunks.title: What is DFA in TOC with examples in hindi 480p
chunks.text: Then we get delta, what is this actually? Transition.

chunks.title: What is DFA in TOC with examples in hindi 480p
chunks.text: What is the meaning of transition?

chunks.title: What is DFA in TOC with examples in hindi 480p
chunks.text: That you applied any alphabet on any state, A or B,

chunks.title: What is DFA in TOC with examples in hindi 480p
chunks.text: where did you go? Means where did you reach? What is its output?

chunks.title: What is DFA in TOC with examples in hindi 480p
chunks.text: At what state will you reach?

chunks.title: What is DFA in TOC with examples in hindi 480p
chunks.text: So this transition is actually called,

chunks.title: What is DFA in TOC with examples in hindi 480p
chunks.text: to transition from one state to another state,

chunks.title: What is DFA in TOC with examples in hindi 480p
chunks.text: that is actually denoted by delta.

chunks.title: What is DFA in TOC with examples in hindi 480p
chunks.text: Then Q note, Q note is your start, start state,

chunks.title: What is DFA in TOC with examples in hindi 480p
chunks.text: means from which state you have started,

chunks.title: What is DFA in TOC with examples in hindi 480p
chunks.text: that is generally our Q note.

chunks.title: What is DFA in TOC with examples in hindi 480p
chunks.text: And what is F? Set of finite final state.

chunks.title: What is DFA in TOC with examples in hindi 480p
chunks.text: Now what is the meaning of saying here?

chunks.title: What is DFA in TOC with examples in hindi 480p
chunks.text: Generally we have final state, we write only one and go.

chunks.title: What is DFA in TOC with examples in hindi 480p
chunks.text: Final state means where your string will be completed,

chunks.title: What is DFA in TOC with examples in hindi 480p
chunks.text: means where your output will be generated,

chunks.title: What is DFA in TOC with examples in hindi 480p
chunks.text: that will be your final state.

chunks.title: What is DFA in TOC with examples in hindi 480p
chunks.text: But many times we have multiple final states also possible,

chunks.title: What is DFA in TOC with examples in hindi 480p
chunks.text: so we generally write this as set of all final states.

chunks.title: What is DFA in TOC with examples in hindi 480p
chunks.text: But all the final states that will be there,

chunks.title: What is DFA in TOC with examples in hindi 480p
chunks.text: remember that how many total states do you have? Q.

chunks.title: What is DFA in TOC with examples in hindi 480p
chunks.text: So means final states will be subset of whose? Q.

chunks.title: What is DFA in TOC with examples in hindi 480p
chunks.text: Obviously final states can't be more than Q,

chunks.title: What is DFA in TOC with examples in hindi 480p
chunks.text: what is Q? Set of all states,

chunks.title: What is DFA in TOC with examples in hindi 480p
chunks.text: all the states are Q.

chunks.title: What is DFA in TOC with examples in hindi 480p
chunks.text: So final state is either maximum to maximum Q,

chunks.title: What is DFA in TOC with examples in hindi 480p
chunks.text: means all are final states,

chunks.title: What is DFA in TOC with examples in hindi 480p
chunks.text: otherwise something less will be there,

chunks.title: What is DFA in TOC with examples in hindi 480p
chunks.text: so you can say that F is a subset of all states, that is Q.

chunks.title: What is DFA in TOC with examples in hindi 480p
chunks.text: Many times these small points can be asked from the point of view of theory in competitive exams.

chunks.title: What is DFA in TOC with examples in hindi 480p
chunks.text: So you should remember these points,

chunks.title: What is DFA in TOC with examples in hindi 480p
chunks.text: because DFA is one of the most important topic of TOC.

chunks.title: What is DFA in TOC with examples in hindi 480p
chunks.text: Because maximum questions in competitive exams are related to DFA.

chunks.title: What is DFA in TOC with examples in hindi 480p
chunks.text: What is DFA? Why do we use it? How do we use it?

chunks.title: What is DFA in TOC with examples in hindi 480p
chunks.text: And designing it later.

chunks.title: What is DFA in TOC with examples in hindi 480p
chunks.text: Which we will discuss in next videos.

chunks.title: What is DFA in TOC with examples in hindi 480p
chunks.text: So here this is the basic definition of these 5,

chunks.title: What is DFA in TOC with examples in hindi 480p
chunks.text: that how do we denote DFA?

chunks.title: What is DFA in TOC with examples in hindi 480p
chunks.text: So we denote it with these 5 symbols.

chunks.title: What is DFA in TOC with examples in hindi 480p
chunks.text: Now we take a simple example.

chunks.title: What is DFA in TOC with examples in hindi 480p
chunks.text: Let's suppose I want to make a DFA,

chunks.title: What is DFA in TOC with examples in hindi 480p
chunks.text: I want to make a deterministic finite automata,

chunks.title: What is DFA in TOC with examples in hindi 480p
chunks.text: for a language, for which language?

chunks.title: What is DFA in TOC with examples in hindi 480p
chunks.text: In which all strings should be starting with A.

chunks.title: What is DFA in TOC with examples in hindi 480p
chunks.text: Means I have to write that language here,

chunks.title: What is DFA in TOC with examples in hindi 480p
chunks.text: in which all strings,

chunks.title: What is DFA in TOC with examples in hindi 480p
chunks.text: because language is a collection of strings,

chunks.title: What is DFA in TOC with examples in hindi 480p
chunks.text: means you should know all those strings,

chunks.title: What is DFA in TOC with examples in hindi 480p
chunks.text: whose starting letter should be A.

chunks.title: What is DFA in TOC with examples in hindi 480p
chunks.text: So if I write its language, how will you write?

chunks.title: What is DFA in TOC with examples in hindi 480p
chunks.text: Which will be the minimum string?

chunks.title: What is DFA in TOC with examples in hindi 480p
chunks.text: The smallest one?

chunks.title: What is DFA in TOC with examples in hindi 480p
chunks.text: It will be A itself.

chunks.title: What is DFA in TOC with examples in hindi 480p
chunks.text: See what is the starting in A?

chunks.title: What is DFA in TOC with examples in hindi 480p
chunks.text: It is A only, so it can't be any smaller than A.

chunks.title: What is DFA in TOC with examples in hindi 480p
chunks.text: Other than this, A can be A,

chunks.title: What is DFA in TOC with examples in hindi 480p
chunks.text: A can be A, A can be A,

chunks.title: What is DFA in TOC with examples in hindi 480p
chunks.text: keep increasing as much as you want.

chunks.title: What is DFA in TOC with examples in hindi 480p
chunks.text: A can be A, B can be A,

chunks.title: What is DFA in TOC with examples in hindi 480p
chunks.text: A can be A, B can be B,

chunks.title: What is DFA in TOC with examples in hindi 480p
chunks.text: A can be A, B can be A,

chunks.title: What is DFA in TOC with examples in hindi 480p
chunks.text: multiple possibilities are there.

chunks.title: What is DFA in TOC with examples in hindi 480p
chunks.text: But can B be A?

chunks.title: What is DFA in TOC with examples in hindi 480p
chunks.text: No, you have to keep this in mind.

chunks.title: What is DFA in TOC with examples in hindi 480p
chunks.text: See, starting letter is B,

chunks.title: What is DFA in TOC with examples in hindi 480p
chunks.text: means these type of strings should not be there.

chunks.title: What is DFA in TOC with examples in hindi 480p
chunks.text: So what is this actually?

chunks.title: What is DFA in TOC with examples in hindi 480p
chunks.text: Start with A.

chunks.title: What is DFA in TOC with examples in hindi 480p
chunks.text: So is this obviously finite or infinite?

chunks.title: What is DFA in TOC with examples in hindi 480p
chunks.text: This is the part of infinite language.

chunks.title: What is DFA in TOC with examples in hindi 480p
chunks.text: Now I have to represent this infinite language

chunks.title: What is DFA in TOC with examples in hindi 480p
chunks.text: with the help of finite automata.

chunks.title: What is DFA in TOC with examples in hindi 480p
chunks.text: How will we do it?

chunks.title: What is DFA in TOC with examples in hindi 480p
chunks.text: Let's start with the designing part.

chunks.title: What is DFA in TOC with examples in hindi 480p
chunks.text: So let's design.

chunks.title: What is DFA in TOC with examples in hindi 480p
chunks.text: First of all, we have Q0, that is our initial state.

chunks.title: What is DFA in TOC with examples in hindi 480p
chunks.text: So starting state or initial state

chunks.title: What is DFA in TOC with examples in hindi 480p
chunks.text: we denote like this, Q0.

chunks.title: What is DFA in TOC with examples in hindi 480p
chunks.text: It can also be multiple.

chunks.title: What is DFA in TOC with examples in hindi 480p
chunks.text: At some places, we denote it in A, B, C.

chunks.title: What is DFA in TOC with examples in hindi 480p
chunks.text: X, Y, Z, so there is no need to worry.

chunks.title: What is DFA in TOC with examples in hindi 480p
chunks.text: We have Q0 as the general notation.

chunks.title: What is DFA in TOC with examples in hindi 480p
chunks.text: And this arrow that we have put here,

chunks.title: What is DFA in TOC with examples in hindi 480p
chunks.text: this arrow tells us

chunks.title: What is DFA in TOC with examples in hindi 480p
chunks.text: that this is the start state.

chunks.title: What is DFA in TOC with examples in hindi 480p
chunks.text: We are starting from here.

chunks.title: What is DFA in TOC with examples in hindi 480p
chunks.text: I want A.

chunks.title: What is DFA in TOC with examples in hindi 480p
chunks.text: Means whenever you design DFA,

chunks.title: What is DFA in TOC with examples in hindi 480p
chunks.text: always remember,

chunks.title: What is DFA in TOC with examples in hindi 480p
chunks.text: design for minimum to minimum string first.

chunks.title: What is DFA in TOC with examples in hindi 480p
chunks.text: After that fill in the blanks.

chunks.title: What is DFA in TOC with examples in hindi 480p
chunks.text: Fill in the blanks means you will understand that.

chunks.title: What is DFA in TOC with examples in hindi 480p
chunks.text: So minimum to minimum is A.

chunks.title: What is DFA in TOC with examples in hindi 480p
chunks.text: Means at least one A should come.

chunks.title: What is DFA in TOC with examples in hindi 480p
chunks.text: So here it is.

chunks.title: What is DFA in TOC with examples in hindi 480p
chunks.text: If one A comes,

chunks.title: What is DFA in TOC with examples in hindi 480p
chunks.text: then we reach which state?

chunks.title: What is DFA in TOC with examples in hindi 480p
chunks.text: We reach Q1 state.

chunks.title: What is DFA in TOC with examples in hindi 480p
chunks.text: So see, A came,

chunks.title: What is DFA in TOC with examples in hindi 480p
chunks.text: so I reached Q1 state.

chunks.title: What is DFA in TOC with examples in hindi 480p
chunks.text: So I started from A.

chunks.title: What is DFA in TOC with examples in hindi 480p
chunks.text: So which is the minimum to minimum string? A.

chunks.title: What is DFA in TOC with examples in hindi 480p
chunks.text: As soon as A comes, I reached Q1.

chunks.title: What is DFA in TOC with examples in hindi 480p
chunks.text: And at Q1, I make it

chunks.title: What is DFA in TOC with examples in hindi 480p
chunks.text: final state. Why?

chunks.title: What is DFA in TOC with examples in hindi 480p
chunks.text: Because as soon as one A comes,

chunks.title: What is DFA in TOC with examples in hindi 480p
chunks.text: it will accept it.

chunks.title: What is DFA in TOC with examples in hindi 480p
chunks.text: As soon as it accepts,

chunks.title: What is DFA in TOC with examples in hindi 480p
chunks.text: our question is being made.

chunks.title: What is DFA in TOC with examples in hindi 480p
chunks.text: Because what we have to make?

chunks.title: What is DFA in TOC with examples in hindi 480p
chunks.text: We have to make such a machine

chunks.title: What is DFA in TOC with examples in hindi 480p
chunks.text: which accepts the strings starting with A.

chunks.title: What is DFA in TOC with examples in hindi 480p
chunks.text: So see, we accepted

chunks.title: What is DFA in TOC with examples in hindi 480p
chunks.text: minimum to minimum.

chunks.title: What is DFA in TOC with examples in hindi 480p
chunks.text: This is the first step of designing.

chunks.title: What is DFA in TOC with examples in hindi 480p
chunks.text: That you make it for minimum to minimum.

chunks.title: What is DFA in TOC with examples in hindi 480p
chunks.text: Now fill in the blanks.

chunks.title: What is DFA in TOC with examples in hindi 480p
chunks.text: What is the meaning of fill in the blanks?

chunks.title: What is DFA in TOC with examples in hindi 480p
chunks.text: Is it DFA? No.

chunks.title: What is DFA in TOC with examples in hindi 480p
chunks.text: Why is it not DFA?

chunks.title: What is DFA in TOC with examples in hindi 480p
chunks.text: What is the reason for this?

chunks.title: What is DFA in TOC with examples in hindi 480p
chunks.text: It is not DFA because

chunks.title: What is DFA in TOC with examples in hindi 480p
chunks.text: in every DFA, if I talk about alphabet,

chunks.title: What is DFA in TOC with examples in hindi 480p
chunks.text: then let's suppose in this question

chunks.title: What is DFA in TOC with examples in hindi 480p
chunks.text: the alphabet we have is A and B.

chunks.title: What is DFA in TOC with examples in hindi 480p
chunks.text: So see,

chunks.title: What is DFA in TOC with examples in hindi 480p
chunks.text: Q0 went here for A.

chunks.title: What is DFA in TOC with examples in hindi 480p
chunks.text: But where is it going for B?

chunks.title: What is DFA in TOC with examples in hindi 480p
chunks.text: So I have to denote

chunks.title: What is DFA in TOC with examples in hindi 480p
chunks.text: for all the alphabets.

chunks.title: What is DFA in TOC with examples in hindi 480p
chunks.text: So Q0 for A is going here.

chunks.title: What is DFA in TOC with examples in hindi 480p
chunks.text: Where will it go for B?

chunks.title: What is DFA in TOC with examples in hindi 480p
chunks.text: If I make B here,

chunks.title: What is DFA in TOC with examples in hindi 480p
chunks.text: then my question is wrong.

chunks.title: What is DFA in TOC with examples in hindi 480p
chunks.text: Because if B comes in the starting,

chunks.title: What is DFA in TOC with examples in hindi 480p
chunks.text: then B will also be accepted.

chunks.title: What is DFA in TOC with examples in hindi 480p
chunks.text: So I can't write B here.

chunks.title: What is DFA in TOC with examples in hindi 480p
chunks.text: What can be the second case?

chunks.title: What is DFA in TOC with examples in hindi 480p
chunks.text: Let's suppose I made a self loop

chunks.title: What is DFA in TOC with examples in hindi 480p
chunks.text: of B. If I make a self loop

chunks.title: What is DFA in TOC with examples in hindi 480p
chunks.text: of B, then see if the string comes

chunks.title: What is DFA in TOC with examples in hindi 480p
chunks.text: BA, then see BA

chunks.title: What is DFA in TOC with examples in hindi 480p
chunks.text: How do you have to run?

chunks.title: What is DFA in TOC with examples in hindi 480p
chunks.text: B comes from the starting.

chunks.title: What is DFA in TOC with examples in hindi 480p
chunks.text: We are on Q0 from Q0

chunks.title: What is DFA in TOC with examples in hindi 480p
chunks.text: and we reached here as soon as A comes.

chunks.title: What is DFA in TOC with examples in hindi 480p
chunks.text: So this is accepted.

chunks.title: What is DFA in TOC with examples in hindi 480p
chunks.text: But we don't have to accept BA.

chunks.title: What is DFA in TOC with examples in hindi 480p
chunks.text: A should come in the starting.

chunks.title: What is DFA in TOC with examples in hindi 480p
chunks.text: So what does this mean?

chunks.title: What is DFA in TOC with examples in hindi 480p
chunks.text: It can't be a self loop and neither can we write B here.

chunks.title: What is DFA in TOC with examples in hindi 480p
chunks.text: So obviously I have to trap B.

chunks.title: What is DFA in TOC with examples in hindi 480p
chunks.text: I have to go to a dead state

chunks.title: What is DFA in TOC with examples in hindi 480p
chunks.text: so that I can't come back from there.

chunks.title: What is DFA in TOC with examples in hindi 480p
chunks.text: So this is called a trap.

chunks.title: What is DFA in TOC with examples in hindi 480p
chunks.text: So we have to make a string

chunks.title: What is DFA in TOC with examples in hindi 480p
chunks.text: We made a state for B

chunks.title: What is DFA in TOC with examples in hindi 480p
chunks.text: Let's say Q0 and whenever B comes

chunks.title: What is DFA in TOC with examples in hindi 480p
chunks.text: it will go to Q0.

chunks.title: What is DFA in TOC with examples in hindi 480p
chunks.text: It can't go anywhere from Q0.

chunks.title: What is DFA in TOC with examples in hindi 480p
chunks.text: There is no path to reach to the final state.

chunks.title: What is DFA in TOC with examples in hindi 480p
chunks.text: This is called a trap.

chunks.title: What is DFA in TOC with examples in hindi 480p
chunks.text: We shifted it to a dead state.

chunks.title: What is DFA in TOC with examples in hindi 480p
chunks.text: So we made it in a dead state.

chunks.title: What is DFA in TOC with examples in hindi 480p
chunks.text: Now see Q0 is going here on A

chunks.title: What is DFA in TOC with examples in hindi 480p
chunks.text: and B is going here.

chunks.title: What is DFA in TOC with examples in hindi 480p
chunks.text: But Q1, where is Q1 going?

chunks.title: What is DFA in TOC with examples in hindi 480p
chunks.text: Q1 is neither made on A

chunks.title: What is DFA in TOC with examples in hindi 480p
chunks.text: nor on B, so there is no problem.

chunks.title: What is DFA in TOC with examples in hindi 480p
chunks.text: You make it simple on A and B.

chunks.title: What is DFA in TOC with examples in hindi 480p
chunks.text: How did it come?

chunks.title: What is DFA in TOC with examples in hindi 480p
chunks.text: How did it come in your mind that you have to make A and B in a self loop?

chunks.title: What is DFA in TOC with examples in hindi 480p
chunks.text: See the starting letter is A.

text: Hello friends, welcome to Gate Smashers. In this video I am going to explain DFA i.e. Deterministic Finite Automata and I am going to discuss all the important points related to it which will be very beneficial for your competitive exams, even for your college or university level exams. So guys quickly like the video, subscribe to the channel if you haven't done it yet and please press the bell button so that you get all the latest notifications. So let's start DFA i.e. Deterministic Finite Automata. Now in the first important point I will tell you what is the meaning of deterministic. What is the meaning of deterministic here is that if I have a state in Automata Q0 and I have applied any string on Q0, I have applied any alphabet on Q0, let's say I have applied A, whether you do A, B, C, any alphabet you have applied then it will go to a particular state. Now what will be that state? It can be Q1, Q2, Qn, Q0 but there will be one state, this is fixed and where this thing gets fixed, we call it deterministic which is not there in the case of NFA because in the case of NFA, it can go to Q1, Q2, Q3, it can go to multiple states, it can go to null state, it can go to any state, so there are multiple choices in the case of NFA but deterministic finite automata means that if you apply any alphabet on a state then it will take you to a particular single state. So this is the meaning of deterministic. And what is Automata? We have already discussed Automata, what we are calling Automata is a machine which is actually using the representation of language. Means if we have to represent language in standard way, whether my language is finite or infinite, if I have to represent it then what I use? I use Automata. So Automata is a machine, an abstract model which actually tells whether this language will be accepted or not. So this is called deterministic finite automata. Now how do we denote DFA? We denote DFA with these 5 symbols, we actually denote it with these 5 tuples. So what we are actually saying here is, first of all Q, what is the meaning of capital Q here? Set of all finite states. Set of finite states means what all states will be there in your machine. What is the meaning of states? That from one state, means starting state to go to output, how many transitions you did, how many states you went to, so why do we call the collection of all those states as Q? Then we have summation here. What is this actually? This is what I have already discussed, this is alphabet, set of alphabet. Let's suppose, what we take commonly, like we take A and B, means where is it going on A, where is it going on B, we take 0 and 1 also but most of the time we use A and B alphabet. Then we get delta, what is this actually? Transition. What is the meaning of transition? That you applied any alphabet on any state, A or B, where did you go? Means where did you reach? What is its output? At what state will you reach? So this transition is actually called, to transition from one state to another state, that is actually denoted by delta. Then Q note, Q note is your start, start state, means from which state you have started, that is generally our Q note. And what is F? Set of finite final state. Now what is the meaning of saying here? Generally we have final state, we write only one and go. Final state means where your string will be completed, means where your output will be generated, that will be your final state. But many times we have multiple final states also possible, so we generally write this as set of all final states. But all the final states that will be there, remember that how many total states do you have? Q. So means final states will be subset of whose? Q. Obviously final states can't be more than Q, what is Q? Set of all states, all the states are Q. So final state is either maximum to maximum Q, means all are final states, otherwise something less will be there, so you can say that F is a subset of all states, that is Q. Many times these small points can be asked from the point of view of theory in competitive exams. So you should remember these points, because DFA is one of the most important topic of TOC. Because maximum questions in competitive exams are related to DFA. What is DFA? Why do we use it? How do we use it? And designing it later. Which we will discuss in next videos. So here this is the basic definition of these 5, that how do we denote DFA? So we denote it with these 5 symbols. Now we take a simple example. Let's suppose I want to make a DFA, I want to make a deterministic finite automata, for a language, for which language? In which all strings should be starting with A. Means I have to write that language here, in which all strings, because language is a collection of strings, means you should know all those strings, whose starting letter should be A. So if I write its language, how will you write? Which will be the minimum string? The smallest one? It will be A itself. See what is the starting in A? It is A only, so it can't be any smaller than A. Other than this, A can be A, A can be A, A can be A, keep increasing as much as you want. A can be A, B can be A, A can be A, B can be B, A can be A, B can be A, multiple possibilities are there. But can B be A? No, you have to keep this in mind. See, starting letter is B, means these type of strings should not be there. So what is this actually? Start with A. So is this obviously finite or infinite? This is the part of infinite language. Now I have to represent this infinite language with the help of finite automata. How will we do it? Let's start with the designing part. So let's design. First of all, we have Q0, that is our initial state. So starting state or initial state we denote like this, Q0. It can also be multiple. At some places, we denote it in A, B, C. X, Y, Z, so there is no need to worry. We have Q0 as the general notation. And this arrow that we have put here, this arrow tells us that this is the start state. We are starting from here. I want A. Means whenever you design DFA, always remember, design for minimum to minimum string first. After that fill in the blanks. Fill in the blanks means you will understand that. So minimum to minimum is A. Means at least one A should come. So here it is. If one A comes, then we reach which state? We reach Q1 state. So see, A came, so I reached Q1 state. So I started from A. So which is the minimum to minimum string? A. As soon as A comes, I reached Q1. And at Q1, I make it final state. Why? Because as soon as one A comes, it will accept it. As soon as it accepts, our question is being made. Because what we have to make? We have to make such a machine which accepts the strings starting with A. So see, we accepted minimum to minimum. This is the first step of designing. That you make it for minimum to minimum. Now fill in the blanks. What is the meaning of fill in the blanks? Is it DFA? No. Why is it not DFA? What is the reason for this? It is not DFA because in every DFA, if I talk about alphabet, then let's suppose in this question the alphabet we have is A and B. So see, Q0 went here for A. But where is it going for B? So I have to denote for all the alphabets. So Q0 for A is going here. Where will it go for B? If I make B here, then my question is wrong. Because if B comes in the starting, then B will also be accepted. So I can't write B here. What can be the second case? Let's suppose I made a self loop of B. If I make a self loop of B, then see if the string comes BA, then see BA How do you have to run? B comes from the starting. We are on Q0 from Q0 and we reached here as soon as A comes. So this is accepted. But we don't have to accept BA. A should come in the starting. So what does this mean? It can't be a self loop and neither can we write B here. So obviously I have to trap B. I have to go to a dead state so that I can't come back from there. So this is called a trap. So we have to make a string We made a state for B Let's say Q0 and whenever B comes it will go to Q0. It can't go anywhere from Q0. There is no path to reach to the final state. This is called a trap. We shifted it to a dead state. So we made it in a dead state. Now see Q0 is going here on A and B is going here. But Q1, where is Q1 going? Q1 is neither made on A nor on B, so there is no problem. You make it simple on A and B. How did it come? A, B, B, whatever you want will be accepted. Our condition is that the first letter should be A and that will be true. And see Q0, you make Q0 simple for A and B. Because if you take it up by mistake or take it here then A will not remain in a dead state. Then someone else can be such an input in which A is not coming in the starting that also gets accepted. So in a dead state, A and B always go on themselves so that we can keep it trapped. We can't come out of it, we can't come up from it nor can we go anywhere else from it. So what is your DFA? Q0 is going here on A and B is going here. Q2 is going here on A and B, this is also fine. Q1 is going here on A and B, so this is also fine. So now your DFA is ready. So this is called transition. Q0, Q1, Q2 and multiply this with submission. In this question, you have which all alphabets A and B. So if you multiply both of them, then what will come? If you multiply both of them then it will come Q0A Q0B Q1A Q1B Q2A, Q2B We have simply cross multiplied both of them. We have cross multiplied both of them. So we have 3 into 2, 6 states. So what we have to do with these 6 states? Now Q. What is Q here? Q0 Q1, Q2 So see how you will represent? Q0A comes. If A comes on Q0Q, then where will you go? You will go on Q1.
